title: Redirect Revalidation, DNS, Address Classes, Proxies, and SSRF · Contract Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for redirect revalidation, dns, address classes, proxies, and ssrf and accepts only compiler plus runtime changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named network, DNS, browser, accessibility, security, load, legal-review, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for connection-time SSRF defense record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Allowlist schemes and ports, reject embedded credentials, and prohibit non-HTTP destinations before resolution.
Classify all IPv4 and IPv6 answers including mapped, loopback, private, link-local, multicast, unspecified, and metadata ranges.
Resolve every Location and reapply authorization, scope, credential, method, DNS, loop, and budget rules per hop.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
